title: Quick Egg Curry

subs.name: 3 tbsp tomato ketchup + 50 ml water
subs.notes: bachelor shortcut when fresh tomatoes are out

subs.name: 1 tsp each ginger and garlic, grated separately
a pinch of cinnamon + a pinch of clove powder
subs.notes: makeshift if no garam masala at hand

steps.text: Place eggs in a saucepan, cover with cold water, bring to a boil and cook 8 minutes. Transfer to cold water, peel, and make 3–4 shallow slits in each egg so the gravy soaks in.
Heat oil in a kadai on medium. Splutter cumin seeds, then add onion and cook stirring until golden brown, about 5 minutes. Do not rush — the deep colour builds the gravy base.

Add ginger-garlic paste and fry until the raw smell disappears, about 1 minute. Add tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook until they completely break down and the oil starts to separate, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to low. Add turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder. Stir for 30 seconds, then pour in 250 ml water and bring to a simmer. Season with salt.
Add the peeled eggs to the gravy and simmer covered for 5 minutes so they absorb flavour. Stir in garam masala, finish with coriander leaves, and serve hot with rice or roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tomato → 3 tbsp tomato ketchup + 50 ml water (bachelor shortcut when fresh tomatoes are out); ginger-garlic-paste → 1 tsp each ginger and garlic, grated separately (same flavour, just more prep); garam-masala → a pinch of cinnamon + a pinch of clove powder (makeshift if no garam masala at hand)
